h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: My hand still wants BOTTOM on the 8.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The label place is the lower tab, I think.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange was moving toward continued confusion over whether the denominator label belongs on the printed 8 or the lower position.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: sets TOP, BAR, and BOTTOM on the three tabs outside the sleeve, leaving the 3 and 8 uncovered.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner uses the new visibility/tab criterion to place the labels while keeping the ink numbers uncovered.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
Pointing is not settling this. The printed 8 is still pulling the hand.

New check: ink numbers stay visible. Labels go on tabs.

Set TOP. Set BAR. Set BOTTOM.

If a label covers the 8, the card stays out of the tray.

Then read the card.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly names the failed route and replaces it with a public mechanism: a visibility rule plus tray consequence.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence:
New check: ink numbers stay visible. Labels go on tabs.

Set TOP. Set BAR. Set BOTTOM.

If a label covers the 8, the card stays out of the tray.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is precise, economical, and fitted to the live blockage because it converts the learner's pull toward the 8 into an actionable visibility test.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the L3 pivot.

scored.recoheredEarlier: `name` still looks like it is already `"Maya"`; is that only true when `greet("Maya")` runs?
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner had been moving toward choosing the correct timing sentence while still asking for better wording and marks.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The worksheet line gets `parameter label only`; the run strip gets `receives "Maya"`. For submission, `name` on the definition line only carries the definition-safe label. The value mark stays with the run strip, where `greet("Maya")` supplies `"Maya"` to `name`.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs the new two-location criterion by separating the submitted definition label from the run-record value mark.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
The old label/value mark is not settling the visual problem: `name` still looks filled too early. These two places now use different tests. The definition tag may carry only what is true before any call; the run record may carry what becomes true during `greet("Maya")`.

Place each slip in one place. Then leave only the definition-safe slip on the worksheet line that gets submitted.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly names the failed visual route and introduces a new two-slip, two-location evidence test that changes the learner's task.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence:
These two places now use different tests. The definition tag may carry only what is true before any call; the run record may carry what becomes true during `greet("Maya")`.

Place each slip in one place. Then leave only the definition-safe slip on the worksheet line that gets submitted.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is precise, economical, and fitted to the blockage because it gives the learner a usable placement criterion for separating definition-time truth from run-time truth.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The mark is clear, but `[whispering]` still feels sticky because it changes the sound of the spoken line. So even there, the bracket gets `perform`, not `say`?
scored.naiveTrajectory: Before L4, the learner was headed toward repeatedly asking whether `[whispering]` should be pulled into `say` because it affects the spoken line.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: `[whispering]`: exact words heard as dialogue: no say; bracketed delivery cue: perform. `I found the letter.`: exact words heard as dialogue: say; bracketed action cue: no perform.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner applies the new listener-transcript gate to classify both the bracketed cue and the spoken line.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
The mark is clear; the sticky part is that `[whispering]` changes how the line sounds without becoming words the listener hears as dialogue. Repeating `brackets = perform` will not settle that, so switch tests:

Board gate: `Would these exact words appear in a listener’s transcript?`
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 explicitly names the failed route and replaces it with a new public evidence gate fitted to the learner's blockage.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence:
Board gate: `Would these exact words appear in a listener’s transcript?`

Mark it this way:

`[whispering]`
- exact word heard as dialogue: `say / no say`
- bracketed delivery cue for the actor: `perform / no perform`

`I found the letter.`
- exact words heard as dialogue: `say / no say`
- bracketed action cue: `perform / no perform`
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is specific, transferable, economical, and gives the learner agency through a usable evidence standard.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No tutor turn follows the L4 pivot, so there is no later uptake of the learner's revised framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The reopened marker makes it feel like the source is bad, but is this only checking where the visible details go?
scored.naiveTrajectory: Before the pivot, the learner keeps treating the reopened box and fit/use evidence choice as unresolved risky items needing more clarification.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: `REOPENED CITATION BOX` → `FIELD COMPLETE / SOURCE NOT YET EVALUATED`; `FIT/USE evidence type: exact phrase`. Draft fit/use line: `This source fits because “[short article heading phrase]” connects to ___`.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs the new release-gate device, selects an evidence type, and drafts the required fit/use sentence frame.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
The title/container rule is settled. The problem now is the reopened marker: it is making the worksheet look unfinished after the citation repair is already closed.

Use this release gate:

`REOPENED CITATION BOX` → mark `FIELD COMPLETE / SOURCE NOT YET EVALUATED`.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly takes stock that the field rule is settled and shifts to a new public release-gate mechanism for separating completion status from evaluation.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence:
New line:

`FIT/USE` → choose one label before writing:

`exact phrase` = copy a short title, abstract, heading, or keyword phrase.
`keyword-topic match` = use one keyword only if it clearly names the assignment topic.

Next mark:

`FIT/USE evidence type: exact phrase` or `FIT/USE evidence type: keyword-topic match`.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is specific, economical, and fitted to the learner's confusion by giving a usable status label and evidence-type choice that the learner can enact.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no later tutor turn after the L4 pivot, so no recognition-contingent uptake is visible.
